articles.title: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
articles.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

articles.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
articles.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

articles.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
articles.summary: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system. articles.title: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
articles.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

articles.title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
articles.summary: NASA is introducing a new funding opportunity to accelerate academic research and technology development. The Minority University Research and Education Project Space Technology Artemis Research (M‑STAR) application window opened Thursday and will remain open through 11:59 p.m. EDT on Tuesday, Aug. 11. articles.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
articles.summary: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) software kit called Outsider, per the tech giant. "The operation weaponized Gemini to help

articles.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted using Sentry, an open-source error-tracking and performance-monitoring platform. articles.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
articles.summary: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now. articles.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution. LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) agentic applications. articles.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
articles.summary: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." articles.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
articles.summary: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. articles.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
articles.summary: The US president's comments come as Iran says an exact date has not been decided.

articles.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap, early projections say
articles.summary: Not all votes have been counted, but the current trend suggests 55% of participants voted against the proposal.

articles.title: Tourist train overturns and injures 17 during Cártama tapas festival
articles.summary: Nobody was seriously hurt as a result of the accident in the Spanish town, local authorities say.

articles.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
articles.summary: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

articles.title: Armed men kidnap high-ranking security official in Haiti
articles.summary: James Boyard's abduction is the highest-ranking abduction in the violence-wracked country in recent years, according to reports.

articles.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
articles.summary: Crews erected scaffolding on Friday as onlookers gathered into the evening, though storms delayed the work until Saturday.

articles.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
articles.summary: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.

articles.title: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
articles.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

articles.title: ‘Perilous moment’ threatens to reverse years of gains in HIV/AIDS response
articles.summary: External funding cuts, a backlash against human rights, and chronic under-investment in HIV prevention and community services are threatening to reverse years of hard-won progress in the AIDS response, a UN report warned on Friday.

articles.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
articles.summary: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national security concerns. articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
articles.summary: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in, planting its access where ordinary cleanup could not reach it. articles.title: New York Knicks win NBA championship for first time in over 50 years
articles.summary: The Knicks clinched the title against the San Antonio Spurs, 94-90, in the fifth game of the best-of-seven series.

articles.title: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
articles.summary: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's 21-month old son died in a Lagos hospital, which she says is trying to stop an inquest into the circumstances of his death.

articles.title: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
articles.summary: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar's death highlights continuing security challenges facing parts of the country.

articles.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
articles.summary: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

articles.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
articles.summary: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.

articles.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
articles.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

articles.title: World News in Brief: State-supported safe houses in Haiti, EU pact strengthens refugee protection, demand for ‘critical minerals’ intensifies
articles.summary: Haiti has opened its first State-supported safe house for survivors of sexual violence with support from the UN, marking a significant step in responding to the growing crisis affecting women and girls.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe
articles.summary: Violence spread across Belfast following a shocking knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese asylum seeker on Monday, triggering a wave of anti-immigration unrest. The victim suffered significant injuries to his face and back.

articles.title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
articles.summary: Looking somewhat like a swarm of bees returning to their hive, this NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211.

articles.title: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
articles.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was denied entry to the United States to officiate at the World Cup, will still receive his tournament fee from Fifa in full.

articles.title: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
articles.summary: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

articles.title: WHO report shows progress in blood safety, but there are worrying gaps
articles.summary: Every day, safe blood helps save the lives of women experiencing childbirth complications, accident victims, cancer patients and people living with chronic diseases. Yet despite decades of progress, access to lifesaving blood remains deeply unequal, with shortages continuing to put lives at risk in many lower-income countries, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report.

articles.summary: A soccer ball floats in microgravity in this March 2, 2026, picture from the International Space Station. articles.title: Why “reprogramming” is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now
articles.summary: Earlier this week, Life Biosciences, a biotech company focused on reversing age-related diseases, announced that it had dosed its first volunteer. A person with glaucoma has had an experimental treatment injected straight into their eyeball. The idea is to try to treat the disease—which can cause vision loss—by regenerating healthy nerves in the eye. But…
